zh.lifeSummary: 當Elisabeth Margaretha Repp於大約1742年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加宾夕法尼亚州貝可斯波 堂史普韋斯特 哈包格時，她的父親Johann Casper Repp27歲，母親Catharina Gebhard21歲。她於大約1761年與Johannes John Rauenzahner在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加宾夕法尼亚州貝可斯結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她在美國賓夕法尼亞州貝可斯住了大約16年。她去世於1823年9月在美國賓夕法尼亞州富蘭克林蒙哥馬利 堂史普享年82歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elisabeth Margaretha Repp于大约1742年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加宾夕法尼亚州貝可斯波 堂史普韋斯特 哈包格时，她的父亲Johann Casper Repp27岁，母亲Catharina Gebhard21岁。她于大约1761年与Johannes John Rauenzahner在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加宾夕法尼亚州貝可斯结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她在美国宾夕法尼亚州貝可斯住了大约16年。她去世于1823年9月在美国宾夕法尼亚州富蘭克林蒙哥馬利 堂史普享年82岁。

nameMeanings.name: Repp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: North German: metonymic occupational name for a ropemaker, from Middle Low German rēp, rep ‘rope’.
German and Dutch: from a short form of the personal name   Reppert  .
Americanized form of Slovenian and Croatian Rep: nickname from rep ‘tail’, probably denoting a man wearing long-tail hairstyle.

variants.name: Reppert
meanings: German: from an ancient Germanic personal name, Ratbert, composed of the elements rād, rāt ‘counsel, advice’ + berht ‘bright’.

nameMeanings.name: Elisabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

variants.name: Elizabeth
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

nameMeanings.name: Margaretha
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaret
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margrethe
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

historicEvents.description: Historical Boundaries 1752: Berks, Pennsylvania Colony, British Colonial America 1776: Berks, Pennsylvania, United States
Historical Boundaries 1752: Berks, Pennsylvania Colony, British Colonial America 1776: Berks, Pennsylvania, United States
Thomas Jefferson's American Declaration of Independence endorsed by Congress. Colonies declare independence.
The Declaration of Independence was signed in Philadelphia on July 4, 1776. The liberty bell was first rung here to Celebrate this important document.

historicEvents.title: Shays' Rebellion
historicEvents.description: Caused by war veteran Daniel Shays, Shays' Rebellion was to protest economic and civil rights injustices that he and other farmers were seeing after the Revolutionary War. Because of the Rebellion it opened the eyes of the governing officials that the Articles of Confederation needed a reform. The Rebellion served as a guardrail when helping reform the United States Constitution.

historicEvents.title: Second State to Ratify U.S. Constitution
historicEvents.description: On December 12, 1787, Pennsylvania ratified the U.S. Constitution.

historicEvents.title: Harrisburg Becomes the State Capital
historicEvents.description: Harrisburg had important parts with migration, the Civil War, and the Industrial Revolution.
historicEvents.urlTitle: United States History-History of Harrisburg Pennsylvania

en.lifeSummary: When Elisabeth Margaretha Repp was born about 1742, in West Hamburg, Bern Township, Berks, Pennsylvania, British Colonial America, her father, Johann Casper Repp, was 27 and her mother, Catharina Gebhard, was 21. She married Johannes John Rauenzahner about 1761, in Berks, Pennsylvania, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 4 daughters. She lived in Berks, Pennsylvania, United States for about 16 years. She died in September 1823, in Montgomery Township, Franklin, Pennsylvania, United States, at the age of 82.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elisabeth Margaretha Repp est née environ 1742, à West Hamburg, Bern Township, Berks, Pennsylvanie, Amérique coloniale britannique, son père, Johann Casper Repp, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Catharina Gebhard, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé Johannes John Rauenzahner environ 1761, à Berks, Pennsylvanie, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 4 filles. Elle a vécu à Berks, Pennsylvanie, États-Unis pendant environ 16 ans. Elle est décédée en septembre 1823, à Montgomery Township, Franklin, Pennsylvanie, États-Unis, à l’âge de 82 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elisabeth Margaretha Repp um 1742 in West Hamburg, Bern Township, Berks, Pennsylvania, British Colonial America geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Johann Casper Repp 27 und ihre Mutter Catharina Gebhard 21. Sie hat um 1761 in Berks, Pennsylvania, British Colonial America Johannes John Rauenzahner geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie hat ungefähr 16 Jahre lang in Berks, Pennsylvania, Vereinigte Staaten gelebt. Sie starb im September 1823 im Alter von 82 Jahren in Montgomery Township, Franklin, Pennsylvania, Vereinigte Staaten.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elisabeth Margaretha Repp è nata il circa 1742 nel West Hamburg, Bern Township, Berks, Pennsylvania, British Colonial America, sua padre, Johann Casper Repp, aveva 27 anni e sua madre, Catharina Gebhard, aveva 21 anni. Lei sposato Johannes John Rauenzahner circa 1761, nel Berks, Pennsylvania, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 4 figlie. Vissuto a Berks, Pennsylvania, Stati Uniti d'America per circa 16 anni. Morì nel settembre 1823, nel Montgomery Township, Franklin, Pennsylvania, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 82 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Elisabeth Margaretha Reppが約1742年にイギリス植民地アメリカペンシルベニア州バークスバーン トウンシュイップウエスト ハンブルグで生まれた時，父Johann Casper Reppは27歳，母Catharina Gebhardは21歳であった。約1761年にイギリス植民地アメリカペンシルベニア州バークスでJohannes John Rauenzahnerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女はアメリカ合衆国ペンシルベニア州バークスに約16年間住んでいた。彼女は1823年9月にアメリカ合衆国ペンシルベニア州フランクリンモントゴメリー トウンシュイップで82歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elisabeth Margaretha Repp(이)가 대략 1742년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 펜실베이니아 주 버크스 버 타운시프 웨스트 햄버그에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Johann Casper Repp(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Catharina Gebhard(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 대략 1761년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 펜실베이니아 주 버크스에서 Johannes John Rauenzahner(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 미국 펜실베이니아 주 버크스에서 약 16년 동안 살았다. 그녀는 1823년 9월 미국 펜실베이니아 주 프랭클린 몽고메리 타운시프에서 82세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elisabeth Margaretha Repp nasceu aproximadamente 1742, em West Hamburg, Bern Township, Berks, Pennsylvania, British Colonial America, seu pai, Johann Casper Repp, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Catharina Gebhard, tinha 21 anos. Ela casou-se com Johannes John Rauenzahner aproximadamente 1761, em Berks, Pennsylvania, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela viveu em Berks, Pensilvânia, Estados Unidos da América por quase 16 anos. Ela faleceu em setembro de 1823, em Montgomery Township, Franklin, Pensilvânia, Estados Unidos da América, com 82 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elisabeth Margaretha Repp родилась около 1742 г. в местности вест хамбург, бирн тоуншип, биркс, Пенсильвания, бритиш колоняль амирика, её отцу по имени Johann Casper Repp было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Catharina Gebhard, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Johannes John Rauenzahner около 1761 г. в местности биркс, Пенсильвания, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 4 дочери. Она жила в местности биркс, Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты около 16 лет. Она умерла сентябрь 1823 г. в местности монтгомири тоуншип, фрэнклинь, Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 82 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elisabeth Margaretha Repp nació aproximadamente 1742, en West Hamburg, Bern Township, Berks, Pensilvania, British Colonial America, su papá, Johann Casper Repp, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Catharina Gebhard, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Johannes John Rauenzahner aproximadamente 1761, en Berks, Pensilvania, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 4 hijas. Vivió en Berks, Pensilvania, Estados Unidos durante unos 16 años. Murió en septiembre de 1823, en Montgomery Township, Franklin, Pensilvania, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 82 años.
